stemHtml: An aircraft is performing a circling approach to land on a different runway. When may the pilot descend below the MDA/H?

options.a: When visual reference is established and can be maintained, the landing threshold is in sight, and the aircraft can carry out a landing with required obstacle clearance assured.
options.b: As soon as visual reference with the aerodrome is established.
options.c: When the aircraft reaches a position from which a geometrically normal descent to the runway can be planned.
options.d: After levelling at MDA/H and flying the downwind leg, commence descent when turning base to intercept the extended centreline.
